metadata.description: subprocess цибхЭЧ: Running external commands: `subprocess.run()` is concise and provides higher-level functionality compared to `Popen`, which offers lower-level control. Using `shell=True` should be done with caution due to injection risks. Python code example for `subprocess.run()`: ```python import subprocess # Running a simple command without shell=True (recommended) result = subprocess.run(['ls', '-l'], capture_output=True, text=True) print(result.stdout) # Running a command with shell=True (use with caution) # result = subprocess.run('echo $SOME_VAR', shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True) # print(result.stdout) ``` Note: The commented-out code using `shell=True` is provided for illustration purposes but should be used carefully to avoid security risks.
